hero.subtitleLine1: A modern, extensible terminal for developers.
hero.subtitleLine2: SSH, SFTP, AI agents, themes, and plugins in one workspace.

terminal.title: Split & Conquer
terminal.description: Tile terminals any way you like — drag, split, resize, and focus.

sshSftp.title: Connect Everywhere
sshSftp.description: Manage SSH hosts in a tree. Transfer files with dual-pane SFTP and chat side by side.

aiAgent.title: AI Built In
aiAgent.description: Chat with Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek — right next to your terminal.

settings.title: Every Detail, Your Way
settings.description: Themes, keybindings, providers, sounds, language — tweak everything in one place.

zeroConfig.title: Zero Config
zeroConfig.description: One launch, done. Auto-configures hooks for Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenCode, and more.

everyAgent.title: Every Agent
everyAgent.description: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenCode, Droid — all agents, one notch, one glance.

terminals.title: Cross-platform
terminals.description: macOS, Windows, Linux. Local terminal, remote SSH, and AI workflows in one native workspace.

soundAlerts.title: Sound Alerts
soundAlerts.description: 8-bit synthesized sounds alert you when agents need attention, even when you are away.

planReview.title: Plan Review
planReview.description: Review agent plans with full Markdown rendering before they execute.

native.title: Built for Developers
native.description: SSH, SFTP, AI chat, theming, and workspace splits — all designed around a real coding workflow.

mcpTools.title: MCP Tooling
mcpTools.description: Connect local or remote MCP servers, inspect available tools, and let agents call them from chat.

sessionRestore.title: Session Restore
sessionRestore.description: Keep terminal sessions and workspace state persistent, then resume quickly after restart.

sshTunnels.title: Advanced SSH
sshTunnels.description: ProxyJump chains, SOCKS5 proxy, X11 forwarding, and trzsz / zmodem transfer are built in.

platforms.question: Which platforms are supported?
platforms.answer: Termlnk is designed for macOS, Windows, and Linux. The website download menu points to GitHub Releases, where new builds will appear as they are published.

agents.question: What AI coding tools does it support?
agents.answer: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, OpenCode, Droid, and more. All sessions appear in one unified notch-style panel.

approval.question: Can I approve permissions without switching away from my workflow?
approval.answer: Yes. When an agent requests permission, the panel expands with Allow and Deny buttons so you can respond quickly without losing context.

data.question: Does my data leave my machine?
data.answer: The product is built around local-first workflows. Session content, terminal state, and approval actions are intended to stay close to your machine and your own toolchain.

setup.question: How does zero-config setup work?
setup.answer: On first launch, the necessary hook entries are written into each supported tool’s config file. A lightweight bridge handles communication between the app and your active coding tools.

resources.question: Does it use a lot of resources?
resources.answer: No. The app is built to stay lightweight while idle, and to surface the information you need without constantly interrupting your focus.

opensource.question: Is Termlnk open source?
opensource.answer: Yes. The project is being opened up, and the website now points downloads to GitHub Releases instead of a pricing page.

changelog.description: Release notes are pulled from GitHub Releases so downloads, dates, and highlights stay aligned with the open-source repository.
changelog.emptyDescription: Published releases from GitHub will appear here automatically.
changelog.errorDescription: GitHub Releases could not be loaded right now. The repository release page is still available.
changelog.title: Changelog
